questionHtml: Explain why the compound C3H8O, produced in (a)(iv), has a higher boiling point than compound C3H6O, produced in d(i).
markschemeHtml:
C3H8O/propan-2-ol: hydrogen-bonding AND C3H6O/propanone: no hydrogen bonding/«only» dipole–dipole/dispersion forces ✔

hydrogen bonding stronger «than dipole–dipole» ✔

meta.title: Explain why the compound C3H8O, produced in (a)(iv), has a higher boiling point than compound C3H6O, produced in d(i).
